heading: Emergency rental assistance
label: 9058c Emergency rental assistance
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021, and not as part of the CARES Act which in part comprises this chapter.Section is comprised of . Subsec. (h) of amended .section 3201 of Pub. L. 117–2section 3201 of Pub. L. 117–2section 9058a of this title
amendments: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021, and not as part of the CARES Act which in part comprises this chapter.Section is comprised of . Subsec. (h) of amended .section 3201 of Pub. L. 117–2section 3201 of Pub. L. 117–2section 9058a of this title

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: 1 Appropriation
children.text: In addition to amounts otherwise available, there is appropriated to the Secretary of the Treasury for fiscal year 2021, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, $21,550,000,000, to remain available until , for making payments to eligible grantees under this section—September 30, 2027

children.label: A
children.text: $305,000,000 for making payments under this section to the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa;

children.label: B
children.text: $30,000,000 for costs of the Secretary for the administration of emergency rental assistance programs and technical assistance to recipients of any grants made by the Secretary to provide financial and other assistance to renters;

children.label: C
children.text: $3,000,000 for administrative expenses of the Inspector General relating to oversight of funds provided in this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: $2,500,000,000 for payments to high-need grantees as provided in this section.

children.heading: Allocation of funds to eligible grantees
children.label: b Allocation of funds to eligible grantees

children.heading: Allocation for States and units of local government
children.label: 1 Allocation for States and units of local government
i
children.text: without regard to clause (i) of paragraph (1)(A);

children.label: ii
children.text: by deeming the amount appropriated under paragraph (1) of subsection (a) of this Act that remains after the application of paragraph (2) of such subsection to be the amount deemed to apply for purposes of applying clause (ii) of ;1section 9058a(b)(1)(A) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “this section”.1

children.label: iii
children.text: by substituting “$152,000,000” for “$200,000,000” each place such term appears;

children.label: iv
children.text: in subclause (I) of such , by substituting “under ” for “under ”; andsection 9058a(b)(1)(A)(v) of this titlesection 9058c of title 15section 9058a of title 15

children.label: v
children.text: in subclause (II) of such , by substituting “local government elects to receive funds from the Secretary under and will use the funds in a manner consistent with such section” for “local government elects to receive funds from the Secretary under and will use the funds in a manner consistent with such section”.section 9058a(b)(1)(A)(v) of this titlesection 9058c of title 15section 9058a of title 15

children.heading: Pro rata adjustment
children.label: B Pro rata adjustment
children.text: The Secretary shall make pro rata adjustments in the amounts of the allocations determined under subparagraph (A) of this paragraph for entities described in such subparagraph as necessary to ensure that the total amount of allocations made pursuant to such subparagraph does not exceed the remainder appropriated amount described in such subparagraph.

children.label: A
children.text: in subparagraph (A), by inserting “of ” after “the amount reserved under subsection (a)(2)(A)”; andsection 9058c of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in clause (i) of subparagraph (B), by substituting “the amount equal to 0.3 percent of the amount appropriated under subsection (a)(1)” with “the amount equal to 0.3 percent of the amount appropriated under subsection (a)(1) of ”.section 9058c of this title

children.heading: High-need grantees
children.label: 3 High-need grantees
children.text: The Secretary shall allocate funds reserved under subsection (a)(2)(D) to eligible grantees with a high need for assistance under this section, with the number of very low-income renter households paying more than 50 percent of income on rent or living in substandard or overcrowded conditions, rental market costs, and change in employment since February 2020 used as the factors for allocating funds.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall pay all eligible grantees not less than 40 percent of each such eligible grantee’s total allocation provided under subsection (b) within 60 days of .March 11, 2021

children.heading: Subsequent payments
children.label: 2 Subsequent payments
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to eligible grantees additional amounts in tranches up to the full amount of each such eligible grantee’s total allocation in accordance with a procedure established by the Secretary, provided that any such procedure established by the Secretary shall require that an eligible grantee must have obligated not less than 75 percent of the funds already disbursed by the Secretary pursuant to this section prior to disbursement of additional amounts.

children.label: I
children.text: rent;

children.label: II
children.text: rental arrears;

children.label: III
children.text: utilities and home energy costs;

children.label: IV
children.text: utilities and home energy costs arrears; and

children.label: V
children.text: other expenses related to housing, as defined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The aggregate amount of financial assistance an eligible household may receive under this section, when combined with financial assistance provided under , shall not exceed 18 months.section 9058a of this title

children.heading: Housing stability services
children.label: B Housing stability services
children.text: Not more than 10 percent of funds received by an eligible grantee from payments made under this section may be used to provide case management and other services intended to help keep households stably housed.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: C Administrative costs
children.text: Not more than 15 percent of the total amount paid to an eligible grantee under this section may be used for administrative costs attributable to providing financial assistance, housing stability services, and other affordable rental housing and eviction prevention activities, including for data collection and reporting requirements related to such funds.

children.heading: Other affordable rental housing and eviction prevention activities
children.label: D Other affordable rental housing and eviction prevention activities
i
children.text: such other purposes are affordable rental housing and eviction prevention purposes, as defined by the Secretary, serving very low-income families (as such term is defined in ); andsection 1437a(b) of title 42

children.label: ii
children.text: prior to obligating any funds for such purposes, the eligible grantee has obligated not less than 75 percent of the total funds allocated to such eligible grantee in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Distribution of assistance
children.label: 2 Distribution of assistance
children.text: Amounts appropriated under subsection (a)(1) of this section shall be subject to the same terms and conditions that apply under paragraph (4) of to amounts appropriated under subsection (a)(1) of such .section 9058a(c) of this titlesection 9058a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Beginning , the Secretary shall reallocate funds allocated to eligible grantees in accordance with subsection (b) but not yet paid in accordance with subsection (c)(2) according to a procedure established by the Secretary.March 31, 2022

children.heading: Eligibility for reallocated funds
children.label: 2 Eligibility for reallocated funds
children.text: The Secretary shall require an eligible grantee to have obligated 50 percent of the total amount of funds allocated to such eligible grantee under subsection (b) to be eligible to receive funds reallocated under paragraph (1) of this subsection.

children.heading: Payment of reallocated funds by the Secretary
children.label: 3 Payment of reallocated funds by the Secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to each eligible grantee eligible for a payment of reallocated funds described in paragraph (2) of this subsection the amount allocated to such eligible grantee in accordance with the procedure established by the Secretary in accordance with paragraph (1) of this subsection.

children.heading: Use of reallocated funds
children.label: 4 Use of reallocated funds
children.text: Eligible grantees may use any funds received in accordance with this subsection only for purposes specified in paragraph (1) of subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: The 50 States of the United States and the District of Columbia.

children.label: B
children.text: A unit of local government (as defined in paragraph (5)).

children.label: C
children.text: The Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa.

children.label: i
children.text: qualified for unemployment benefits; or

children.label: ii
children.text: experienced a reduction in household income, incurred significant costs, or experienced other financial hardship during or due, directly or indirectly, to the coronavirus pandemic;

children.label: B
children.text: 1 or more individuals within the household can demonstrate a risk of experiencing homelessness or housing instability; and

children.label: C
children.text: the household is a low-income family (as such term is defined in .section 1437a(b) of title 422So in original. A closing parenthesis probably should precede the period.2

children.heading: Inspector general
children.label: 3 Inspector general
children.text: The term “Inspector General” means the Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Unit of local government
children.label: 5 Unit of local government
children.text: The term “unit of local government” has the meaning given such term in .section 9058a of this title

children.heading: Availability
children.label: g Availability
children.text: Funds provided to an eligible grantee under a payment made under this section shall remain available through .September 30, 2025
